exploration_log.hindsight: Start with inlining the prefix check in innerfind from the start; the delegation to findNext obscures the traversal semantics.

key_decisions.decision: Inline the prefix matching loop directly in innerfind after the findNext call
key_decisions.reasoning: Inlining makes the propagation explicit at every recursive level while keeping findNext intact for Traverse; the additive approach avoids regressions

solve_notes: The `innerfind` closure in `Find` delegated all prefix matching to `findNext`, which already checks `EnablePrefixMatching`. The fix makes prefix matching explicit inside the recursive traversal body itself: after the exact-match call to `findNext` returns nil, `innerfind` now directly iterates children and applies `hasNameOrAliasPrefix` at every recursive level. This ensures prefix comparison is unambiguously propagated to all depths of the command tree.
task_text:
Fix Command.Find not respecting EnablePrefixMatching for deeply nested subcommands

When `EnablePrefixMatching` is set to `true`, prefix matching works for
top-level subcommands but fails for commands nested more than two levels
deep. The `Command.Find()` method in `command.go` does not propagate
prefix matching through the recursive `innerFind` traversal. Fix the
traversal to apply prefix comparison at every level of the command tree.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: argsMinusFirstX
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: used in the recursive innerfind call; agent needs to understand arg stripping semantics to correctly propagate the prefix-matched arg removal

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient_defs contains the edited method and the helper it calls. thrash_preventing adds findNext (so the agent understands existing prefix logic and avoids duplicating or conflicting with it) and argsMinusFirstX (so the agent understands how remaining args are passed to the recursive call). Without seeing findNext, an agent might add prefix logic in the wrong place or duplicate it incorrectly.
